2605.10794.title: Can You Keep a Secret? Involuntary Information Leakage in Language Model Writing
2605.10794.paper_summary: Language models are deployed in settings that require compartmentalization: system prompts should not be disclosed, chain-of-thought reasoning is hidden from users, and sensitive data passes through shared contexts. We test whether models can keep prompted information out of their writing. We give each model a secret word with instructions not to reveal it, then ask it to write a story. A second model tries to identify the secret from the story in a binary discrimination test. The secret word never appears literally in any output, but all five frontier models we test leak it thematically -- through topic choice, imagery, and setting--6hy-at rates significantly different from chance, up to 79\%. When told to actively hide the secret, models write \emph{away from} it, and this avoidance is itself detectable. The leakage is cross-model readable, scales sharply with model size within two model families, and disappears entirely for short-form writing like jokes. Giving the model a decoy concept to ``focus on instead'' partially redirects the leakage from the real secret to the decoy. Attending to a secret appears to open up an information channel that frontier LLMs cannot close, even when instructed to.
2605.10794.paper_summary_zh: 語言模型被部署在需要隔離的環境中：系統提示不應被披露，思考過程對用戶隱藏，敏感數據在共享上下文中傳遞。我們測試模型是否能將提示的信息排除在其寫作之外。我們給每個模型一個秘密詞，並指示它不要透露，然後要求它寫一個故事。第二個模型試圖在一個二元區分測試中從故事中識別出這個秘密。秘密詞在任何輸出中都不會字面出現，但我們測試的所有五個前沿模型在主題上都會洩漏它——通過主題選擇、意象和設定——其洩漏率顯著高於隨機機率，最高可達79%。當被告知要主動隱藏秘密時，模型會寫作\emph{遠離}它，這種回避本身也是可檢測的。這種洩漏在不同模型之間可讀，並且在兩個模型系列中隨著模型大小的增加而急劇上升，對於像笑話這樣的短文寫作則完全消失。給模型一個誘餌概念以“轉而專注於”部分地將洩漏從真實秘密轉向誘餌。關注一個秘密似乎打開了一個信息通道，前沿的LLM無法關閉，即使在被指示的情況下也是如此。
2605.10794.author: Ari Holtzman et.al.
